registration center welcome to registration center please use any of below forms to register or update your data with nigerians in diaspora organisation germany e v all boxes may be filled please pay the registration fee immediately to complete your registration euro for individual euro for corporate membership acknowledgement and bank details will be sent to you we thank you for your interest in nido now please choose any form from below to register individual registration corporate registration
how do i become a member of nido simple just fill the forms and return them to the secreteriate if you qualify to be a member of nido the secretary will inform you and on the payment of a registration fee of euro you become a member you can also become a member by attending nido meeting you will be asked to introduce yourself there and then you will be told if you qualify as a nido member or not if you are qualified you will be registered immediately you can pay the registration fee then or later
constitution of all saints chapel university of benin benin city whereas we christians belonging to the protestant denominations of the university of benin and its environs are agreed that it is meet right and a duty to worship in fellowship the lord god almighty through his son jesus christ for which purpose the all saints chapel university of benin has been established and whereas we acknowledge that in such a chapel we need to administer our affairs in an orderly lawful and respectable manner in accordance with god s revealed will in the holy bible and in the spirit of tolerance love and accommodation which is the hallmark of the protestant tradition we therefore in pursuance of these ideals and of the welfare of all persons in the chapel on the principle of freedom equality and justice do hereby make enact and give to ourselves this constitution chapter i general provisions this constitution may be cited as constitution of all saints chapel university of benin benin city this constitution shall come into force on january in this constitution unless the context otherwise requires or permits the following words or expressions shall have the following meanings a chapel year shall mean january to december b convener shall mean the person who summons a meeting c misconduct shall mean any conduct which is prejudicial to the good name or reputation of the chapel or which undermines the proper administration of the business of the chapel misconduct shall include corruption dishonesty stealing adultery embezzlement assault false claims against the chapel slander negligence of duty suppression of information falsification or unauthorised destruction of accounts or records d priest shall mean a trained and ordained minister of god e protestant denomination shall be understood as all christian communions not belonging to the roman catholic church f senior member shall mean a member who is thirty years or more in age g university shall mean university of benin benin city nigeria in this constitution words importing the masculine gender shall include the feminine gender the aims and objectives of the chapel shall be to a provide a forum for the corporate participation of christians in divine worship in the university- of benin b promote and entrench the ideals of practical christian living as enunciated by our lord jesus christ c foster an attitude of fervent study of the bible and a habit of seeking searching for and understanding the word of god through the organisation of christian fellowship meetings and d engage in the work of evangelism and discipleship within and outside the university the chapel shall be named known and addressed as all saints chapel university of benin benin city hereinafter referred to as the chapel branches at university of benin teaching hospital ubth and ekehuan campus are to be known as ubth worship centre and ekehuan campus branch respectively membership of the chapel shall consist of individuals who personally willingly and by conviction accept jesus christ as lord and saviour all such members shall have their names registered in the chapel register provided a they pay not less than the amount fixed as chapel dues or b they are active in the programmes of the chapel or c they pay their tithes regularly the chapel services shall be open to all members of the public all communicants of the protestant denomination are welcome to partake of the holy communion members shall faithfully perform their spiritual physical moral and financial duties and obligations to the chapel chapter ii congregation there shall be a chapel congregation which shall consist of registered members the congregation shall a be the supreme governing body of the chapel b have powers to make policies and regulations for the chapel c have powers to give necessary directives to any officer or organ of the chapel pursuant to the provisions of this constitution d elect members of the chapel committee and such members of sub-committees as provided for in this constitution except the chaplain and the assistant chaplain s and e receive and consider annual reports of the chairman secretary treasurer and auditor there shall be three types of meetings a a general meeting to be known as the annual meeting of congregation amc which shall be held once in every twelve months normally in the month of february but not exceeding six weeks thereafter the amc shall i receive and consider annual reports of the chairman secretary treasurer and the external auditor and ii elect members of the chapel committee except the chaplain and assistant chaplain s b emergency meeting of congregation which may be summoned by the chairman as the need arises at his discretion or by a notice signed by at least members of congregation served on the chairman and a copy pasted on the chapel notice board c an extraordinary meeting of congregation emc shall be summoned by the chairman of the caretaker committee for the purpose of electing a chapel committee in the event of its premature dissolution the date time and agenda of any meeting shall be determined by the convener s of the meeting all meetings of congregation shall be held on sundays within the chapel premises notice of the annual meeting of congregation shall be given not less than three consecutive sundays before the meeting is held notice of an emergency meeting of congregation shall be at the discretion of the convener s notice of an extraordinary meeting of congregation shall be given not less than two sundays before the meeting is held no business shall be transacted at any meeting unless a quorum is formed quorum at a congregation meeting shall consist of of or registered members whichever is less no business shall be transacted at any emergency meeting of congregation save such as shall have been notified in the notice of the meeting the chairman shall preside at every meeting of congregation except at an extraordinary meeting in his absence the deputy chairman shall preside before a motion duly moved and seconded becomes a resolution of the congregation it must have received the support of a simple majority of those present voting shall be by a show of hands in the case of equality of votes the chairman of the meeting at which the show of hands takes place shall be entitled to a second or casting vote the right to vote and be voted for shall be limited to members only chapter iii chapel committee there shall be a body known as the chapel committee the chapel committee shall consist of the following a chairman who shall be the chaplain b deputy chairman who shall be a lay person c assistant chaplain s d priest-in-charge ekenhuan campus branch e all serving priests f chapel secretary g assistant secretary h treasurer i financial secretary j assistant financial secretary k assistant financial secretaries students male and female l publicity secretary m people s warden n legal adviser o choirmaster p sunday bible study superintendent q superintendent of children s department r internal auditor s building sub-committee chairman t harvest sub-committee chairman u members elected from congregation of whom shall be students v members elected from ekehuan campus congregation w youths presidents ugbowo and ekenhuan campuses x immediate past deputy chairman and secretary subject to the provisions of section of this constitution the chapel committee shall be responsible for the following a making of rules for the day to day running of the affairs of the chapel b collection of revenue expenditure of money for and on behalf of the chapel c over-all control and superintendence of chapel properties movable and immovable d appointment welfare and discipline of chapel employees e co-ordination of the duties and responsibilities of all the sub-committees of the chapel f preparation and recommendation of annual budget to congregation and g appointment of members of the sub committees of the chapel unless otherwise stated in this constitution the chapel committee shall meet once every month in addition to any other meeting that may be necessary quorum shall be one third of the attendance with the exception of the chaplain assistant chaplain and member priests members of the chapel committee shall be elected by congregation at the annual meeting of congregation amc and shall hold office for a period of two years except the chairman of the harvest subcommittee who shall hold office for one year but shall thereafter not be eligible for further election to the same office until two years have elapsed following the end of the single term the chairman shall preside at all meetings of the chapel committee and the budget sub-committee the deputy chairman shall assist the chairman as appropriate and act in his place whenever the chairman is absent or otherwise unable to perform his duties the chaplain shall a give spiritual leadership in the chapel b be responsible for the arrangement of divine services in the chapel and for other uses to which the chapel may be put c be responsible for the day to day administration of the chapel and d co-ordinate the activities of priests who are members of or associated with the chapel the assistant chaplain s shall assist the chaplain in the performance of his duties and act for him in his absence except in his capacity as chairman of congregation the chapel committee and the budget sub-committee the chapel secretary shall a serve as secretary of congregation chapel committee budget sub- committe and any other committee as the congregation or chapel committee may direct and b summon meetings as directed by the chairman and take minutes of such meetings the assistant chapel secretary shall assist the chapel secretary in the performance of his duties and serve as secretary in his absence the treasurer shall a be responsible for keeping the financial accounts of the chapel and b receive all monies accruing to the chapel from the financial secretary and bank same within two days from the date of receipt the financial secretary shall be responsible for a the receipt of all monies collected at chapel services and other occasions and for all dues levies etc and b keeping proper records of all monies collected and shall hand over such monies to the treasurer not later than days from the date of receipt the assistant financial secretary shall assist the financial secretary in his duties the assistant financial secretary student shall assist the financial secretary particularly in collecting dues levies etc from the students the publicity secretary shall publicize the activities of the chapel the people s warden shall a represent the congregation in matters affecting the chapel as the occasion arises b supervise the ushers in their functions and c present the offerings to the officiating priest for blessing the legal adviser shall give legal advice the choirmaster shall a be responsible for organizing and maintaining an active choir and b ensure that all the equipment of the choir is safe and in good working condition always sunday bible study superintendent shall be responsible for the coordination and overall superintendence of sunday bible study superintendent of children s department shall be responsible for the co-ordination and overall superintendence of children s department serving priest s shall perform any duty or duties that may be assigned to them when the need arises by the chaplain chapel committee or congregation
the xxx university of xxx i the course format or housestyle the course guide the course guide cg should precede all other units of the course the cg gives a general overview of the course and provides useful information to the student about the structure or format of the course the credit units attached to the course and how they are to be obtained assessment assignments and examinations and the schedule of activities important dates the unit a unit is the smallest segment of an instructional material that can stand alone but which is also linked to other units to form a module it should have the following characteristics i it should engage an average distance learner dl student for at least three hours of serious work which is equivalent to one hour of work under the conventional system ii depending on the subject matter a full unit of structured content should cover between and pages a typeset or points of times roman or any appropriate font iii a unit should have a a table of contents toc b introduction c objectives d main content which should have self-assessment exercises saes and self-assessment questions saqs the introduction may also contain some saes and saqs particular symbols or icons may be adopted to signal theses activities in the text e conclusion f summary g references and recommended books iv at the end of the unit there should be at least standard tutor-marked assignment question tmaq which will form part of the tmaqs al the end of the module v tables diagrams illustrations should be used where appropriate the module i contains up to units which make up a module the units which make up a module must be thematically linked to each other ii at the end there should be tmaqs there will be an assignment file which will contain all the tmaqs at the end of every unit iii there should be references and a list of recommended books at the end of each module the course the course is made up of all the modules
mathematics paper code - tuesday th november noon p m national business and technical examinations board national business and technical certificate examinations mathematics paper ii essay paper code - time hours minutes instructions attempt nine questions in all all candidates should attempt all the questions in section a all candidates except secretarial and business candidates should attempt four questions from section b secretarial and business candidates are to attempt two questions only from section b and any other two questions from section c in each question all necessary details of working including rough work must be shown with the answer as accurate as tables and data allow non-programmable and cordless calculators are allowed the following may be provided for use in the examination a graph paper b drawing paper for construction work c mathematical and statistical tables and formulae
national business and technical examinations board nabteb national headquarters ikpoba hill p m b benin city edo state manual for station officers for nabteb certificate examinations a introduction the conduct of public examinations demands high fidelity quality control in order to ensure quality assurance in content and delivery this comes up at various stages in the examination process namely during the pre-examination actual conduct and post-examination activities one of such important stages in the actual process of conducting an examination is at the post of the custodians b rationale for station officers at the custodians question papers and answer scripts are held in trust for examination bodies if not carefully handled the issuing of question papers and receiving of answer scripts can pose serious threats to the quality of the examinations at this level for example there have been reports of a delay in issuing out question papers by custodians who are very busy with their primary assignments b some custodians being tempted by unscrupulous supervisors to issue out question papers which are not due on the time table c failure of custodians and supervisors to faithfully and honestly fill the movement form for the collection of question papers and return of answer scripts thereby creating difficulty for the board in resolving naughty cases where these records are to serve as pointers there is therefore the need to designate a person at each custodian whose duty it is to see to the timely issuance of question papers to and receipt of answer scripts from the supervisors among other responsibilities for the purpose of nabteb certificate examinations such a person is called a station officer c functions of station officers station officers are to issue out question papers to supervisors and receive answer scripts from them ensure that no supervisor collects question papers earlier than necessary i e not earlier than one hour before the commencement of the paper ensure that supervisors submit answer scripts to the custodians not later than one hour after the end of a paper check and ensure that parcels containing question papers are correct in number and are not tampered with ensure that answer scripts of each centre are kept in separate bags ensure that mark sheets for practical papers are returned to the custodian by the supervisor ensure that all unused parcels of question papers are returned intact to the custodian ensure that the movement book is correctly filled by the supervisors make replacement for any absentee supervisor if necessary and contact the zonal office or national headquarters immediately check the movement book to ensure that all mark sheets in respect of the practical question papers collected have been returned where this has not been done the affected supervisors must be directed to do so ensure that script envelopes are properly labeled before accepting them from the supervisors ensure that arrangement is made to replace a supervisor who may wish to discontinue with the supervision ensure that unused materials are parceled and returned to the board guidelines to station officers question papers are to be released to supervisors not earlier than hour before the commencement of each paper all question papers released to supervisors must be entered and signed for by the supervisors in the movement book indicating time and date of collection station officers are to use the delivery note with the custodian to do stock taking of the question papers station officers should submit a comprehensive report on the station work to test administration department not later than hours after the completion of the assignment ensure that supervisors submit their reports and claims on the last day of the examination as directed ensure that supervisors produce their letters of introduction before commencing official transaction with them station officers are to report at their respective custodians one and half hours before the commencement of the st paper of the day
moderation exercise a well conceived test item must pass tests a the test of expert judgement b the test of use the test of use is the pretest trial test which is essential to provide considerable information for statistical analysis of data the facility value and discrimination index are the most useful of all the statistics obtainable from this analysis the use of these statistics in the determination of the specific items to be included in a question paper or be rejected is the act of moderation or item analysis this is a quantitative approach to the evaluation of test items on the other hand the test of expert judgement in a more subjective approach- it is the vetting or shredding of the items this is a qualitative approach to the act of moderation criteria to be used in item vetting shredding what section of the syllabus does the item test year topic what ability thinking level does the item test a b c d e is the language mathematics science sound or technically correct i e is the content of the question correct language-wise etc are there clues to the key examine the length or the clue grammatical inconsistencies specific determinants or other writing defects what is the key are the detractors plausible is there more than one defensible answer is it reasonable to expect an average examinee to answer the item in the allotted time where appropriate are the responses in the correct sequences can the wording be improved by eliminating unnecessary details over sophistication factoring out when item vetters have subjected the items to these rigorous examination the level of acceptability of each item can be determined if a major amendment is required on an item to enable it meet with the criteria laid down then that item should be rejected where necessary amendments can be made on items that are defective they should be carried out to make them conform to the prevailing criteria attached herewith is a checklist for vetting and moderating both multiple choice items and essay practical type items are options which are names arranged alphabetically are the options overloaded are repetitive parts included in the stem d the key does the key answer the questions does it stand out from the rest is it the only answer to the question asked are there words in the stem that may give out the key is the key to one item given in another item when both are given in the same test e distractors is the distractor plausible or sharp enough is there any other correct option does any one of them attract unnecessary attention editing essay practical type items the following points should be noted when writing essay practical type items a the question i must be within the syllabus ii must be concise clear and unambiguous iii should be structured directly so that the candidates do not have to guess iy if subdivided should be arranged in logical order v the number of points for which full marks will be given should be indicated e g state four advantates of preferable to discuss the advantages of vi indicate the type of response expected from candidates e g calculate solve write draw vii the length of the answer expected should be indicated in the wording of the question e g list name state suggest few words or brief answers while describe indicate longer answer calculate wants more accurate answers than sketch words like write note on discuss etc leave the candidates in doubt as to the length and depth of answer required and should normally be avoided viii the use of the word you should normally be avoided however on your diagram label is acceptable ix any necessary information should be given in the question rather than in the rubric as candidates will forget e g tt is x the use of trade names should be avoided xi diagrams symbols abbreviations units and terminology used in questions must conform to the current standards abbreviations and conventions in use xii adequate time for the average candidate to write the answer to the question xiii each questions must be of the same standard as that of other questions if there is a choice xiv each question must be moderately difficult and should be able to separate the very good candidates from the average and the very poor ones xv question should not contain anything to give undue advantage to any religious tribal or ethnic group xvi questions should not be expensive to develop use diagrams if only necessary to clarify the questions the solution marking scheme i solutions and marking schemes must accompany all draft questions ii numerical questions should be accompanied with full solutions iii the marks awarded for each part of the question must be indicated iv do not award marks for writing ability neatness or presentation unless they are essential to the subject being assessed moderation of essay items the moderating committee must thoroughly go through each item and the marking scheme to ensure that the i item is syllabic ii item is workable and can be completed within the same time given iii language of the item is within the reach of the majority of the candidates for whom it is meant iv item is clearly worded and unabiguous v item is not a verbatim repetition of a past item vi marking scheme is adequately comprehensive vii allocation of marks to point is fair to all candidates neither too stingy nor too generous each test item should be amended by the moderators in accordance with i vii above every moderator should reflect all such amendments on his her copy items that are so bad that they can not be salvaged should be rejected and must be so indicated on every moderator s copy to ensure that they are not selected in error onto the final test paper moderation of objective items each objective item moderated must ensure that i item is within the scope of the syllabus ii the item is clearly worded and unambiguous iii there is an agreement of the item with the options in tense preposition and number iv there is only one key v all the distractors are plausible and are derivable from possible common errors on the topic vi the length of the key does not give it out vii the key is not too frequently placed in any one position viii the item is testing only one thing at a time ix the alphabets of the options a-e are not used to label appended diagram x options involving numbers and figures are arranged in either ascending or descending order xi the item is workable within the time given xii the item contains no hidden trick item editing items should be edited by subject officer s as soon as the items are received from the examiners an item editor must be a subject expert as well as be familiar with the techniques of item writing items may be accepted modified rejected or referred to the writer for amendment an item editor edits items to ensure that i their structure does not contain any mistake and conforms to the principles outlined below ii their position in the table of specification is adhered to iii they test the intended ability recall comprehension application analysis synthesis evaluation iv there is only one correct option in multiple choice item editing multiple choice items a multiple choice item comprises a stem and options the options comprise plausible distractors and the key the following points should be considered for editing purposes a the item as a whole is the item testing what it is supposed to test is there a better way of testing what the item tests is it within the scope of the syllabus is it worth asking is it clearly stated or is it ambiguous for the level of candidates does the item have the test specification number indicated does the diagram or graph agree with the stem and options does the item test too many things at once are there vague expressions are there any double negatives b the stem does the stem clearly define the aim of a specific problems does it contain unneccessary information or does it need more information can it be better or clearly worded is it technically correct if unavoidably worded in the negative is the negative term written in capitals does the stem start with which one of the following instead of which of the following c the options are the options similar in structure and length semantecallyand grammatically do they overlap do they complete the stem are numerical ones arranged in order of magnitude

obafemi awolowo university ile-ife february resolution of the governing council on the workers action in the university the governing council at its meeting held on february and noted and reviewed the activities of some of the workers in the university who had since june most of the time abandoned their duties it noted in particular the action of the workers who on tuesday february held the vice-chancellor and the registrar hostage for more than five hours with threat to their lives on wednesday february physically assaulted members of the governing council on their way to council meeting and subsequently held them hostage from pm to about pm until they were rescued by men of nigerian police force led by the area commander of the ife area command of the police on the same day vandalized some of the vehicles that were to convey members of council back to their residences after the days meeting and assaulted some of the drivers on thursday february again prevented the efforts to remobilize the vehicles that were vandalized the previous day until the police men under the area command intervened council while appreciating the right of workers to legitimately make demands for their welfare however was of the view that resorting to brigandage and criminality in orchestrating such demands is inimical to the progress of the university it affirmed that such acts of brigandage and criminality are unexpected should not be tolerated in an academic community and in any community for that matter and should therefore be avoided council averred that in an academic community workers are expected to demonstrate the highest ethos of civility by communicating their demands in writing ensuring constructive engagement council consequently resolved that all manners of rallies by workers during office hours is prohibited henceforth all workers should immediately resume work and stay on their duty posts for all official duties failing which appropriate disciplinary measures will be applied the unions should adhere strictly to extant laws on trade disputes council then directed that the unions involved should be surcharged for the cost of the repairs of the vehicles that were vandalized all the members of staff found to have been involved in the acts of brigandage and criminality should be made to go through due disciplinary process council affirmed its commitment to staff and students welfare and the overall wellbeing of the university as an academic community it assures all workers that it will continue to be opened to democratic and legitimate channels for quick resolution of grievances it appealed to all workers to recommit themselves to the ideals of the university with due diligence council also calls the attention of all members of staff to the need to comply fully to the code of conduct for the university community registrar and secretary to council
obafemi awolowo university staff club rules regulations for the swimming pool users the swimming period for non-members of the staff club terminates at - pm every swimming day the prescribed fee should be paid and shower should be taken before entering the pool swimmers in swimming costumes are restricted to the pool and garden area any erring student will be summarily ejected and made to appear before the university disciplinary committee except for the members who can swim nobody should swim in the absence of the life guard any child of years and under should be accopanied by an adult who will take care of him her before allowed to swim trainee swimmers are restricted to the shallow area of the pool any such persons who go to the deep end do so at personal risk and if seen will be summarily ejected the club reserves the right to fix the number of swimmer in the pool at any time student swimmers are prohibited from smoking in the club premises and from purchasing cigarattes student swimmers are not allowed access to alcoholic but to buy snacks and soft drinks from the bar and kitchen student swimmers would not bepermited to make use of other games within the club premises competition organise by the club might be an excpetion swimmers are warned against reckless diving the so called power dive and others trick the club will not take responsibility for any mishap that result from failure swimmers are prohibited from eating drinking or dropping their soft drinks bottles within the imeediate vicinity of the swimming pool such substances as broken bottles damage the swimming pool filtration clearing systems generally the club reserved the right to expell any individual from club premises should he she be judge guilty of any indiscipline in addition individuals be expelled will be reported to the appropriate university authority
strike bulletin the technical committee tc of the dialogue committee finished its work on wednesday may and produced a signed report inspite of the series of obstacles placed in its way the report of the technical committee was scheduled for presentation to its principal the dialogue committee yesterday thursday may unfortunately the minister of education did not show up the meeting therefore could not discuss the report and was adjourned the honourable minister of labour has appealed to the union to be patient as he is making effort to reconvene the meeting of the dialogue committee next week to discuss the report our union remains committed to the resolution of the crisis through the ongoing dialogue and would attend any re-scheduled meeting accordingly national strike coordinating committee nscc is aware of the announcements of resumption in some universities we salute the resoluteness of our members in the affected branches and urge members in other branches not to be deceived by such announcements the struggle continues government has obviously become more desperate-a lot of people have been and are being massively recruited to play various roles including putting our loyal members under pressure and blackmail to break the strike with our collective resolve they will continue to fail for victory belongs to us and the nigerian people colleagues our struggle has reached a critical stage and we must remain resolute what to do demand from your chairman a copy of the full briefing on the struggle so far and digest same for your information also get a signed copy of the report of the technical committee from your chairman do not sign any register or resumption form in whatever format presented by your university administration the strike remains total do not attend any statutory or other meetings however disguised do not teach but continue to do your research attend all congress meetings for first-hand information from your chairman worse attempts will be made in the coming days at propaganda and disinformation by government and its agents don t be deceived be patient with our detractors do your best to educate them on the issues at stake continue to educate the general public colleagues the stakes are high in this struggle remain resolute persevere this union makes us strong national strike coordinating committee may

structure of the university membership professor chairman convenor professor o member professor member mr k secretary preamble on th february council approved the proposal to set up a committee to review the committee system in the university in order to make it more effective and efficient council nominated three of its members to serve on the joint committee and requested senate to nominate three if its members on the th march senate nominated three of its members to serve the joint committee the report of the joint committee was considered by council on st february and the new committee structure recommended was approved on the apparent realization of the fact that senate s input was required on the matter the joint committee s report was forwarded to senate on the th september senate decided to set up an ad-hoc committee with the following terms of reference to consider the committee structure as presented determine its efficiency and effectiveness make modifications where necessary and advise senate as appropriate method of work the senate committee henceforth referred to as the committee held its inaugural meeting on friday th april three other meetings were held on th may and st june and st june and th august the committee reflected on the terms of reference given to it discussed the procedural issues that led to council approving the joint committee s report before seeking the input of the senate and checked the enabling provision of the university laws to undertake the review of the committee structure the committee then studied the report of the joint committee along with the existing committee structure as contained in the university handbook it thereafter developed its own structure general observations a as the business committee of the senate earlier observed senate s input should have been sought on the joint committee s report before the council could consider it let alone approve it the chairman of the senate may need to assure the senate that the reviewed structure prepared by the joint committee and approved by council without senate s input is not being implemented already b in considering the report the ad-hoc committee noted that the legal backing for the establishment of committees in the university was provided as section of the university main laws which states as follows the council the senate the board of any faculty and any body of persons established by or in pursuance of the provisions of this law may respectively establish such committee for the purposes of their respective functions as they may think fit c it was also noted that the joint committee did not indicate in its report the justification for merger modification cancellation or retention of any committee as recommended in the report the reader was therefore left to do the tedious deductions conclusions and recommendations the committee has diligently performed the duties assigned to it and came up with the findings suggestions and recommendations contained in the attached papers it is hoped that these recommendations would improve the dynamic committee systems of our great institution name signature professor chairman professor respect of dr and mr and deliberated as follows i dr promotion from the post of lecturer i to senior lecturer the meeting noted and observed from the cv submitted that dr had more publications to his publications to his credit after his last promotion it also noted that he had acquired his m phil degree and he s preparing for his ph d qualifying examination and that he has satisfied the three years minimum requirement for promotion based on the fact that mr had been performing his duties excellently the review panel recommended that he be promoted from the post of lecturer i to the post of senior lecturer with effect from st october ii cases for normal annual increment having carefully studied all the cv s submitted the following observations were made i some of the journals were making steady progresses in the programmes iii some cvs does not give complete information in the area of publications iv some cvs have some typographical errors or were not properly arranged according to university format v that some members did not even submit their cv s for review after an extensive deliberations on each of them the departmental review panel made the following recommendations i those who had some corrections to make on their cvs should do so and forwarded the corrected cvs to the departmental office for further necessary action ii that staff should always ensure that their publications are published in reputable local and international journals iii that those on ph d programmes should speed up action as the passing of it is a key criteria to move from one level to the other iv that members of staff who did not submittheir cv s for review because of the erroneous belief that they were not due either for promotion or confirmation of appointment should submit their cv s for record purposes v the departmental review panel then having noted all the staff are making steady progress in their ph d programmes and are performing excellently well in guidelines for authors hinweise fur autoren the editorial committee of the natog-journal cordially invites authors to send their articles for the next issue however we would like to request you to follow our guidelines for publication in preparing your article your manuscript should be submitted on a floppy disc and an accompanying printout you should use a format mm x mm or in x in please indicate on the floppy disc the type of computer and word processing programme including version that you are using and the file name of your manuscript the floppy disc will be returned to you as soon as possible however we can not accept liability for manuscripts of floppy discs your text should be double-spaced or with a left margin of inch cm and a right margin for proof marks quotations in the text should be single-spaced adding an additional left margin of about in cm book titles as well as important key words should be in italics bibliographical references in the text should read like this wie maier f sagte wie schon seit langem bekannt ist maier after a quotation maier a the a after the year indicates that maier published another article in which you are also quoting remember to include such letters in your bibliography for easy reference the last number refers to the page s footnotes should not be used for bibliographical references but for additional notes or comments that you want to make please use arabic numbers raised above the line the footnote itself should be placed at the bottom of the page your bibliography following the text should be arranged alphabetically and detailed as possible including the name of the publishing company and or series title and number for the benefit of all readers the editorial committee of the natog-journal will accept articles in german as well as in english it reserves the right to reject or return manuscripts for revision thank you for your co-operation

library plan of the library the library consists of the north and south wings which are connected by walkways on two levels membership membership of the library is available on completion of a registration card to all students members of the senior staff of the university and such other persons as may be determined by the library committee or the university librarian on behalf of it students are required to renew their registration at the beginning of each academic year library cards and borrower s tickets are not transferable books issued on them remain the responsibility of the person whose name appears on them a lost library card or borrower s ticket may be replaced upon submission of a written application the library collection the hezekiah olimasanmi library now contains over volumes it consists of two main areas i the undergraduate areas and ii the research areas serials collection the serials collection consists of i current journals the most current issues of which are shelved in the display section of the serials room a latest back files i e the latest years of journals which are on open access to registered senior staff and postgraduate students b older back files i e journals older than ten years are on closed access to all categories of readers who must obtain and complete request forms at the serials section africana special collection the africana special collection is a collection of rare and other books of primary interest to people whose fields of interest are in african studies staff publications and theses submitted for higher degrees of the university as well as of other universities are also housed there the collection is of closed access documents collection the documents collection includes official publications ot the federal government of nigeria the old regional governments the present state governments and the federal capital territory it also includes publications of other african governments and international organizations reference collection dictionaries encyclopedia handbooks directories atlases university calendars etc are shelved in the reference room bibliographies indexes and abstracts are available in the bibliography room reference books do not ordinarily circulate a newspaper clippings file post-october and a vertical file of reprints and other pamphlet type material is kept in the reference room reserve collection i day reserve collection multiple copies of textbooks particularly some of those recommended for specific courses are shelved in the reserve books room on floor north wing east ii two-hour reserve some other materials periodical articles in particular are placed on -hour reserve these may be obtained on request signature and seal number required and retained for a period of two hours at a time subject to renewal provided other readers have not demanded the materials recent accessions a selection of books added to the library stock is normally displayed for several days before being put in the main collection the books may not be borrowed while on display but may be reserved at the loans desk catalogues a library catalogue is a finding list of books and other materials available in the library the following catalogues can be found in the catalogue hall i the author title catalogue it the subject catalogue iii the shelf list iv the serials catalogue v the documents catalogue how to borrow a book when you have found the book you want to borrow you will be required to sign your name and address on the book card provided in duplicate you must surrender a borrower s ticket for each book borrowed when you return a book you must ensure that you receive your borrower s ticket back immediately reservation a book can be reserved by filling a reservation slip in which case it will not be renewed for the present borrower when returned and if it is already overdue it will be recalled at once inter-library loan if the book you require is not in stock it is often possible to borrow it from another library this service is dependent on goodwill and cooperation between libraries and readers who benefit from it are required to observe the regulations applying to each loan photocopying services within the limitations imposed by copyright the library is able to supply readers with photocopies of periodical articles and parts of books at moderate charges penalties for overdue or lost books penalties for overdue books will be imposed as follows a n per day for the first days thereafter all loan privileges will stop b books specially recalled by the university librarian will attract a fine of n per day after the third day from the date of recall c books lost or damaged will attract a fine five times the current cost of the books d no student will be allowed to attend the graduation ceremony or receive his her certificate without a clearance certificate from the university library to the effect that no book or fine is outstanding against him or her division of students affairs guidance and counseling unit the division of student affairs has professional counselors who are committed to helping students grow in self-understanding in the process of integrating their personal and academic experiences the services are free to students and are confidential i e not used as part of his her other university records the services include personal counseling group counseling study skills improvement test anxiety reduction personal crisis intervention psychological testing career and occupational counseling and settlement of grievances between students where necessary consultations are made with campus organizations specialist and academic departments to ensure that students problems are resolved satisfactorily the counselors can be contacted in rooms of the division of student affairs between a m and p m monday to friday scholarship and financial assistance the division of students affairs serves as a link between students and sponsoring authorities both within and outside nigeria students are advised to check the notice boards in their respective faculties as well as those at the division of student affairs building for advertisements and other relevant information liaison is also maintained between students and governments at various levels for scholarship and bursaries relevant sections of university examination regulations i e registration for university examinations absence from examination examination offences and penalties registration for university examination a a candidate for a university examination must have registered for the courses in the prescribed format not later than the closing date prescribed for registration for such courses any candidate who fails to register for courses at the appropriate time as prescribed by senate will not be allowed to take any examination in such courses any examination taken without course registration shall be null and avoid b students who register for courses are committed to the number of units registered for and are expected to take examinations in such courses if a student failed to take an examination he would be scored of c any student who does not have any course or courses to offer in a particular semester should apply for leave of absence d a candidate who has less than units in a particular semester to graduate should apply to his her faculty board for permission to register for less than units failure to do so constitutes a breach of regulations which may result in the non-processing of the candidate s results e a candidate who can not register for courses during the prescribed period for registration because of an illness must ensure that a medical report on his illness is forwarded by him or his parents sponsors to reach the dean of his faculty not later than four weeks after the end of the normal registration period as scheduled in the university calendar such a medical report should be forwarded for authentication by the director of medical and health services for it to be considered valid such a candidate shall be exempted from the penalties of late registration all applications should be routed through the head of department f students must attend a minimum of of course instructions including lecture tutorials and practicals where required to qualify to sit for the examination in any course g a candidate for a university examination in a particular degree programme should not be a regular candidate for another degree in this or any other university concurrently any candidate so discovered shall forfeit his her studentship absence from examination candidates must present themselves at such university examinations for which they have registered candidates who fail to do so for reasons other than illness or accident shall be bound by the following regulations a any student who fails to register for courses during one semester without permission should be deemed to have scored o f in the minimum number of units required for full time student i e units b candidates who registered for courses attended classes regularly did all practicals and tests but did not take required semester examinations should be given a continuous assessment grade in each of the affected courses and a grade of in the examination which they should have taken but which they did not take c candidates who have less than units to graduate but who fail to take the required examinations should be deemed to have scored of in the outstanding courses only provided such candidates obtained permission to register for less than units d any candidate who on account of illness is absent from a university examination may be permitted by the senate on the recommendation from the appropriate faculty board to present himself for such examination at the next available opportunity provided that i a full-time student in the university shall report any case of illness to the university health centre at all times ii when a student falls ill during examinations he should first report to the director medical and health services before attending any hospital outside the university a report of sickness should be made to the registrar within a week and a medical certificate for validation of his illness within three weeks iii when a student falls ill before an examination he shall be under an obligation to send a medical report countersigned by the director medical and health services within one week of such illness any time outside this period shall be considered on its own merit iv the director of medical and health services should within hours submit a medical report on a candidate who is ill during an examination and is taken to the health centre or referred by it to the hospital for treatment v a candidate applying for leave of absence on medical grounds must forward his application together with a medical report to the dean of his faculty through his head of department the medical report must be countersigned by the director of medical and health sciences all applications for leave of absence must be taken by the appropriate faculty board
report of the may fgn xxx dialogue committee meeting preamble in the last report on the state of the work of the technical committee tc which was constituted by the fgn dialogue committee certain problems that hampered the work of the technical committee were identified also certain areas in which no consensus had been reached and or where agreement could not be reached on how to record the differences of positions of the two teams government and were indicated the technical committee therefore adjourned to enable the members of both the government team and the team to consult to allow time for reflection on how to proceed and also to enable the chairman of the technical committee brief as well as consult the chairman of the dialogue committee the honourable minister of labour employment and productivity the tc also agreed to resume its work on may if the chairman of the dialogue committee considered it useful to have the tc to proceed with and complete its work the decision of the tc to leave to the chairman of the dialogue committee the issue of whether or not it should continue and complete its work was informed by the government team s insistence that the tc had come to an unbreakable deadlock and could make no further progress especially on one of the key issues namely the scheduling of the specific amounts in the agreement and the related issue of the specific amount to be provided to make the universities functional when academic work resumes in contrast team was optimistic that a way could be found to resolve the impasse meeting of the technical committee with the minister of labour as it turned out the honourable minister of labour employment and productivity by his letter of april invited the chairman and members of the tc to a meeting with him at a m on may he also summoned a meeting of the dialogue committee for may at p m the special meeting of tc was presided over by the honourable minister of labour employment and productivity it was attended by all the members of the team plus all the asuu teams technical advisers the chairman of the tc and his two technical advisers her excellency mrs and chairman of the committee of vice-chancellors in attendance also were the permanent secretary and director in charge of trade union matters federal ministry of labour the executive secretary of nuc as well as the secretariat staff of the tc the government team in the meeting had two of its three members as well as one of the technical advisers to the team namely the one drawn from the federal ministry of finance it was at this special meeting of the tc presided over by the honourable minister of labour that the team made a proposal which broke the impasse on re-scheduling of the specific amounts of funds required by the universities as contained in fgn agreement of june the team proposed that the following clause should be inserted in the report of the tc that in the event of the federal government having any difficulties in meeting its obligations as contained in the schedule of funds to be provided to universities in any year should be invited to enable the two sides work out mutually acceptable solutions the government team then agreed to provide a schedule of the specific amounts of required funds as contained in the june fgn agreement the understanding that the clause would be inserted into the report with this positive development the tc withdrew from the meeting and then resumed its normal meeting promising the chairman of the dialogue committee and the honourable minister of labour that it would finish its work and submit a report by pm on may to enable the dialogue committee hold its meeting as already planned the resumed meeting of the technical committee the leader of the government team had at the meeting with the honourable minister of labour and chairman dialogue committee which ended at about am on may requested for a two-hour interval this was to enable him consult his principals and prepare a schedule acceptable to them before presentation to the tc his request was granted and the tc therefore agreed to resume by pm on the same day when the tc resumed at p m on may with its work which was mainly to approve a draft report distributed to members a week earlier it was delayed by one strange development the government team produced what it titled schedule of required funds and defined required funds in the fgn agreement to imply that the federal government was not under any obligation to fund federal universities in line with the amount specified in the agreement rather the federal government would provide whatever amount it wished or could afford and the rest would be provided by the private sector parents civil society organisations etc also in the paper on schedule of required funds presented by the government team was inserted after the schedule a clause totally different from the one mutually agreed upon in the meeting with honourable minister of labour and productivity the two clauses contained in the paper on scheduling by the government team which tended to contradict or undermine the provisions on funding in chapter five of the fgn agreement of are reproduced below for ease of reference clause b on page of government team s paper from the above provisions the government team notes as follows b that the levels of funds indicated under a recurrent expenditure - b capital expenditure - represent a summation of the required funds by the universities which come from all sources including government private sector internally generated funds funds expended by parents in the maintenance of their children etc it was therefore not intended to come from government exclusively another clause on page of government team s paper in the case that in any year government is unable to achieve the desired quantum of funding as scheduled the executive secretary on behalf of the honourable minister of education shall invite and explain to them what efforts have been made and the inability of government to meet the scheduled funds for the year shall understand and shall not use this as a basis fo a strike the chairman of tc urged the leader of the governemnt team to modify the first clause so as not to create the impression that governemnt was interpreting the fgn agreement in a manner that freed government of any specific financial obligations to the universities the chairman fo cvc urged the government team to remove that clause altogether since it made the schedule of required funds prepared by the government team totally meaningless team also asked the government team to delete the first clause and modify the second one in the manner already mutually agreed upon in the meeting with honourable minister of labour the government team rejected the pleas and requests on the ground that it could not on its own alter a document prepared with the consent and approval of its principals when it became obvious that once more a deadlock had been reached on this point the tc decided to adjourn its meeting and resume the following day to facilitate the work of the tc the chairman proposed and the two sides agreed on the following first a sub-committee made up of two members from each team that is i e four members including the team leader in each side should meet early the following day and agree on the text of the report including the aspect of it that reflected government s position on scheduling of funds second the sub-committee should also reflect the amendments to the draft report earlier agreed upon by the tc third the financial implications or all aspects of the agreement as determined by another sub-committee set up earlier should be inserted in the text of the report the tc in the hope that much of the work would be done at the sub-committee level therefore decided to reconvene at p m on may to approve the report sign it and submit it to the minister of labour it was the hope of the tc that between p m and p m hrs it would be able to produce the final clean copy of its report the tc did not meet this target for the following reasons first the government team did not agree to modify its submission on schedule of required funds until about p m thereafter it was incorporated into the draft report second the government team questioned the amount provided by the subcommittee on financial implications concerning the quantum of funds required to pay the approved salary including the arrears the government team put the amount required at less than n actually n while the sub-committee on financial implications put the figure at n for the period may to december the government team s table did not specify the number of months the matter was then referred to the tc for resolution third and most important on the one hand all the members of the team including its technical advisers were present and punctual two members of the government team and one technical adviser were also present and punctual representing the minister of education was however conspicuously absent mrs member of board of governors was present and on time on the other hand the chairman of the tc did not contrary to his habit come to the meeting in time indeed he was not there until p m about minutes to the scheduled p m time for the beginning of the meeting of the dialogue committee the explanation he gave for his unusual lateness was that despite his frantic calls from his hotel to the no vehicle was provided until that late in the day the leader of the government team a director in the was fully aware of his position he explained it is on record that before the incident the had on all occasions provided a vehicle for the chairman of the committee for his movement to and from his hotel punctually it was only when the chairman of the tc arrived that the process of approving and producing the final clean copy of the report was commenced the report was ready by about p m

an improved solar cabinet dryer with natural convective heat transfer introduction solar energy is fast becoming an alternative source of energy because of the high rate of depletion of the conventional energy sources like thermal chemical and petroleum energy the quest to utilize the cheap and readily available solar energy and also to overcome the shortcomings of open sun drying process brought about the development of solar drying system to improve upon the existing food and agricultural products preservation techniques dehydration or drying is a simple and low cost way to preserve crops that might otherwise spoil drying of food crops is an energy intensive operation high prices and shortages in fossil fuel increase the emphasis on using solar energy as an alternative source of energy in non-industrialized tropical countries like nigeria where sunlight is abundant natural sun drying is one of the commonest methods of drying drying is generally carried out by direct exposure of the food crop to solar radiation in order to reduce moisture and thereby reducing weight drying preserves nutritional quality of food reduces the risk of contamination by toxic mould and disease carrying insects the use of solar energy to dry crops is nothing new in tropics as many edible and even cash crops such as cocoa and coffee beans have traditionally been dried on racks placed in the sun although the traditional sun drying process is common and widely embraced by all however the process is slow and sometimes incomplete under unfavourable climatic condition often the drying products are subjected to noxious effects of dust dirt and insect infestation as a result of inadequacies of the open sun drying process research effort on drying agricultural produce have been on the increase over the years in order to develop and produce an economical effective and systemized method of drying wilson bamiro proposed a new formula for determination of solar radiation that was shown to give good agreement when applied to the analysis of annual daity insolation on a seasonal basis the formula predicted a linear relationship between the daily diffuse radiation and the daily total radiation in line with the liu and jordan model but with the diffuse component generally higher in qualitative agreement with observations in the tropics this study was particularized for ibadan nigeria in the study by fagbenle it was revealed that total solar radiation increases with latitude in nigeria but the month of august experiences the least total solar radiation throughout the country with values ranging between and mj m -day depending on the location in another study fagbenle enumerated two methods used for estimating total solar radiation and recommended maximum-likelihood quadratic fit for use in nigeria it has been observed that the solar intensity ranges from to w m with a total incident energy per day of to mj m in llorin nigeria of latitude on lasode the optimum collector tilt angle for sites in nigeria with latitude on or less is latitude angle while for locations with latitudes greater than n is latitude angle o fagbenle the major drying parameters pertinent to bed drying method are the temperature and relative humidity of the drying air its airflow rate and the depth of the bed in the drying chamber these are parameters that determine how fast or slow drying will proceed inside the solar cabinet dryer basunia and abe a lower relative humidity provides a better drying potential a high humidity inhibits the rate of drying the increase in the temperature of the drying air cause a reduction of its relative humidity and thus increases its moisture carrying capacity the increase in air temperature increases the temperature of the food products being dried and this in turn causes the vapour pressure within the food crop to increase without any change in vapour pressure of the drying air a high vapour pressure difference is thus set up which improves the drying potential of the solar cabinet box hence the hotter the air the more moisture it will hold before becoming saturated the most important factor in any successful drying process is the rate of airflow in and out of the dryer a high velocity air in addition to taking moisture will also sweep it away from the drying product s surface thus preventing moisture from creating a saturated atmosphere is strictly needed in natural convective heat flow drying processes in designing any natural convection drying system great emphasis is placed on the depth of bed in the drying chamber since no external airflow delivery mechanism is involved this is to allow for the static pressure head developed by a given airflow delivery to overcome the resistance offered by the bed so that air flow could occur a proper bed depth ensures that there is no pressure drop across the bed and that enough air flows over the food crop on the bed in the drying chamber of the solar cabinet dryer basunia and abe lasode designed solar dryer with collector that could transmit w m thermal power to the drying air the dryer could raise the temperature of ambient air from oc to oc and reduce relative humidity from to tests later carried out on the dryer revealed that at the early stage of drying the samples in the open sun dried faster than that in the solar dryer but at the later stage the rate of drying in the solar hot box became better than the open sun on the overall the samples in the solar dryer show no sign of mould formation lighter colour and better taste compared with that of open sun the focus of this work is to improve on the study of lasode by increasing the depth of the bed in the drying chamber improving the air flow rate by providing adequate ventilation and increasing the thermal power of the drying air the variables and mechanisms involved in improving the dehydration processes and the design parameters and considerations involved in the construction of this solar cabinet dryer are detailed psychrometry and drying process consider the ambient air at ta oc and of relative humidity r h φa at the location -ilorin on nigeria from the ihve psychrometric chart see figure if the dryer is to heat air to a temperature tb oc the relative humidity of the air would reduce to about φb if moisture is removed from blanched yam of moisture content wet basis with this heated air until an equilibrium relative humidity of φc is attained then the temperature of the drying air will be reduced to tc oc drying with heated air at constant rate is a constant wet bulb process described by path b to c in figure the absolute humidity changes from to the change in the humidity ratio of heated air δwh is thus δwh - that is every kilogram of dry air entering the dryer will pick up kg of water the drying path for unheated air is described by ad its corresponding humidity ratio change is then δwc by implication the capability of the heated air for dehydration is nearly fourteen times greater than ambient or unheated air the amount of energy absorbed by the air when being heated from state point a to b is calculated as the difference in enthalpy hb- ha between the two points δh hb- ha kj kg this is the quantity of thermal energy needed for dehydration from point b toc for the drying process the energy balance equation is where mr is the mass of water evaporated from the drying sample and absorbed by the drying air of mass ma tb and tc are the initial and the final temperature of the drying air respectively ca is the specific heat capacity of air while l mj kg is the latent heat of vapourization for free water at oc design consideration in designing a solar cabinet drying system the following point are considered a amount of moisture to be removed mr from a given quantity of crop to bring the moisture content to a storage level in a specified time which is calculated from the equation below where mi is the initial mass of the drying sample mf and m are the final and initial moisture content for storage on percent -wet basis respectively b total drying time this is the daily sunshine hours for which drying will take place the annual average daily useful sunshine hour in llorin nigeria is hr dms - thus the average drying rate dr t is the total drying time required to remove mr kg of water from the wet produce average drying rate is determined from the ratio of mass of moisture to be removed by solar heat and drying time c quantity of air needed for drying the quantity of air needed for drying can be calculated either from the energy balance equation or by using psychrometric chart the problem in designing a solar dryer is to determine a suitable initial temperature ta i e the temperature of the drying air before passing through the bed and the correct quantity of air mass o air ma necessary to remove a specified amount of moisture mr from the wet produce the volume flow rate of air required for drying qa is given by basunia and abe as where qa is the volume flow rate of air required for drying m s and p represent the density of air kg m mass ma of air required to remove mr kg of water is calculated thus d daily solar insolation and the useful energy needed by the solar cabinet dryer this is considered in order to determine the useful energy to be received by the dryer and also to determine the area of the solar collector that is needed to trap such energy a south facing tilted collector when compared to other orientations gives maximum daily solar energy collection in the northern latitudes it is the exact opposite in southern latitudes where it must be north-facing the optimum tilt angle is made equal to the latitude of the location-ilorin in order to optimize solar collection by the collector following sayigh the solar energy collected on a tilted surface is obtained by multiplying the horizontal solar radiation with the tilt factor rb the tilt factor is the ratio of the beam radiation flux falling on a tilted surface to that falling on a horizontal surface consider a tilted surface facing south i e when ϋ surface azimuth angle where δ -angle of declination θ -angle between an incident beam of flux and the normal to the plane σ -latitude β- the slope ŵ- the hour angle ϋ -surface azimuth angle and θ -angle of incident on a horizontal surface the average monthly rb factor ranged from in october to in november basunia and abe hence total useful energy eu in kj needed to evaporate moisture is calculated from
