the classical or traditional grammar was the grammar of the ancient greeks latin this grammar was case based that is the function of the word varies in a sentence as whether a subject an object or an indirect object this means that the function of a word is based on the appropriateness of the inflections english for specific purposes emerged after the classical or traditional grammar or description model and as such the description model did not have or rather does not have a strong hold on esp english for specific purposes however there are contributions of this description model that esp largely drew from the first contribution of this description model is the register analysis which is among the development of esp the notion that language varies according to context gave rise to the need of a particular language for a particular language situation register analysis therefore proves relevant in designing a syllabus for a particular language context however the content of a syllabus can not be solely based on the register analysis of the particular field in that there can be similarities between one field and another take for instance the teaching of simple sentences compound sentences can be included in the syllabus of a science subject like chemistry so also simple and compound sentences can also be included in the syllabus of grammar for english students what this means is that although register analysis is relevant in designing a syllabus for various fields or subjects it should not be the only basis for selecting and sequencing items in a syllabus another contribution of the classical description model is in the area of structures and forms that is the structures and forms of a language was introduced by this model and it s description by the model is still being alluded to english for specific purposes also draws from this as learners are being taught the structure and forms of the language in order to fully understand the language mere teaching the language or a language without teaching its structure and forms is like teaching only what the language can do without teaching what basically holds the language that is the structure and form serves as the basis which should not in any way be neglected the structural description or structuralism came after the classical or traditional description and it proved useful to a language like english which is a word-ordered language its contributions to english from which esp draws from is the slot and filter form which can also be referred to as the substitution table here words are arranged in a way that some are left to be arranged that is learners are asked to find substitutes to the words arranged they are to include words that can be used in place of the original word and then arrange them accordingly this will enable them to think and carry out the task another contribution of the structural description is the structural syllabus in the structural syllabus items to be taught are arranged in the form of the simple and immediately used ones are taught before the complex ones this means that those forms or items that will seem relevant or useful to the immediate needs of the learners are taught before those that are not needed immediately teaching the simple items before the complex ones will enable learners learn faster and easily as learners approach a field with items that they are more familiar with this will also make them interested in the teaching process that is it will ignite their motivation to learn structural description also introduced the surface level of language description which deals with the competence performance one has in a language that is it is the surface level that accounts for the performance of a learner in a language though the surface level accounts for the performance but something enables the surface level to be able to account for the performance and this is the deeper level of meaning it is of good importance when one has proficiency in a language but it is also of great importance if one or the learner knows the rules that enable him or her to be able to generate rules from the deeper level thus the grammar of a language is not the surface level of a language but the rules that enables learners to generate the surface level from the deeper level the structural description however did not lay much emphasis on the deeper level but only the surface level of meaning english for specific purposes esp is not a variety of english but rather an approach to language learning in which all decisions as to content and method are based on learners reasons for learning hutchinson english for specific purposes esp is not a variety of english but rather an approach to language learning in which all decisions as to content and method are based on learners reason for learning hutchinson et al the above quotation simply states what esp is about that is the essence of esp is to meet the specific needs of the learners esp asserts needs and strategies motivation in which subject matter and relevant skills are taught that is the needs of the learners are found out and met the motivation of the learner is required to do this and the particular skill s the learner needs are taught general english is more like the english everyone is taught the basics of the language the structure the forms that make up the language that is the rudiments of the english language general english is more of the academic purpose that is it is usually learnt in school it is taught to students english for specific purposes esp and general english share some similarities in that learners of esp and general english must or should know the rudiments of the language esp assumes that its learners must have a knowledge of the language they are only in an esp class because they need a skill to be proficient and efficient in their place of work esp learners are professionals who as i mentioned earlier needs english to meet their specific needs another similarity between esp and general english is that in either case a particular skill is taught in general english all the four skills of listening speaking reading and writing are taught but in esp a particular skill is taught it still boils down to the fact that a skill is being taught whether all or one english for specific purposes esp and general english also differ in some areas esp learners are intermediate or adult learners who are learning english to improve their proficiency and efficiency in their place of assignment while general english learners are pupils students who are learning english in order to be competent in the language this shows that while esp is geared towards commercial purposes organising seminars for learners general english is geared towards academic purposes esp centers on language in context it is particular about the context where the learners will use the language it is geared towards making the learners excel well in their area of speciality general english therefore is aimed at making the learners good users of the language using it to excel in their academic pursuit and good communicators in the language in english for specific purposes esp a particular skill is taught to the learner this is the skill that the learner needs to succeed in his her place of jurisdiction the skill can either be a speaking listening writing or reading skill the reading skill however is seldom taught to esp learners in general english on the other hand all the four skills of reading speaking listening and writing are taught to the learners this is because they are expected to be an all rounder that is to know and excel in all the skills esp is usually learner centered as against general english that is language centered that is it is the learner that is the focus of the esp expert the learner is what is at the back of his mind how to teach the learner and enable him or her to excel and for his or her needs to be met in the general english on the other hand it is the language that is paramount to the teacher how to teach the language to the students to make them comprehend it esp learners approach the study field through the areas that they are familiar with the interest of the learners is ignited once they see the need use of the learning learning to meet their immediate needs general english is however not for immediate needs it can be for future needs while general english is taught in school esp can be taught in halls hotels as long as the environment is conducive for learning a the origin of esp three factors are responsible for the origin of esp and they are a brave new world a revolution in linguistics a focus on the learner a brave new world the end of the second world war in brought about an age of unprecedented expansion in the technical economic and scientific activity which was unified by two dominating forces technology and commerce the unrelenting progress of these two forces saw the need of an international language this role however fell on english prior to this time people learnt a language without asking why they were learning it they just learnt the language for the justification of learning it people however saw the need for english as it was the language of the international currency a new generation of people then came about as they saw the importance to learn english business men and women learnt in english so as to carry out their business successfully doctors learnt english so as to write prescriptions accordingly secretaries learnt english so as to write memos and letters well the oil crisis of the s also contributed to this development as their was a mass flow of funds into the western and oil rich countries english then became a big time business as people saw it as meeting their required goals a revolution in linguistics linguistics has traditionally been accustomed to describing the features of a language but the attention however shifted to communication uses that is how language is used for communication the guiding principle of esp however became tell me what you need english for and i ll tell you the english you need a focus on the learner the educational psychology at the until the th century shifted to the learner the interests of the learner was taken into cognisance the whole essence of the language was therefore based on the needs of the learner b target needs target needs are the needs a learner needs in order to function well in a target situation the linguistic features of the target situation should be taken note of and aimed at in order for the learner to carry out his or her duties in the target situation the target needs are divided into three they are necessities lacks wants necessities are what is needed in the target situation that is what is in the target situation that the learner doesn t have lacks are what the learner needs to succeed in the target situation that is what is missing in the learner wants are what the learner requires in the target situation and they should not be overlooked

english for specific purposes esp is an approach to english language teaching in english as a second language esl and english as a foreign language efl environments that is dedicated to teaching learners specifically what they need to learn to function well in their various areas of specialization over time it would be important to note that different models of language description have helped or contributed to the development process of esp the notable description models by language scholars were the traditional or classical model the structural model the transformational generative grammar model amongst others the classical or traditional model is a model of language description that word took after the methods of describing the classical languages namely latin and greek at that time those scholars saw these classical languages as the ideal and the description of any other language should follow the patterns of the description of these languages it must be noted however that these classical languages are case-based languages and that the focus of their description were based on the function of the individual words within the sentence which are used with their appropriate inflections so the description of english followed suit for instance the description of the sentence below would take the pattern of the identification of the individual functions of the words in the sentence as put thus my brother killed the bird character subject verb object the significant contribution of this model of description to esp is in that esp has retained most of the terminologies utilized in the description of the language terminologies like subject verb object noun verbs pronouns e t c take their source from the classical description model in addition to this another contribution of this model to esp is in that esp also retained the use of cases words in the teaching of grammar so also the use of inflections to determine concepts like time and tense shortly after the reign of the earlier described model the scholar bloomfield came up with his own proposition to challenge the methods of the classical description model in the s he posits that the english language could actually be described in terms of the syntagmatic and paradigmatic relationships which exist within the sentence so a change of any of the words within the sentence or a change of its structure could lead to the generation of a different sentence this argument was based on the belief that unlike the character the cased-based nature of the classical languages the english language is a word-order language the significant contributions made by this model of description is the act of viewing the language as a structure where the elements within are related the introduction of the substitution table as a means of teaching the english grammar is another notable contribution made by this description model to esp this table made use of the slot-and-filler character method introduced by this model to teach learners how to produce grammatically correct utterances in english another notable contribution of this model to esp is the introduction of the structural syllabus in the teaching of language this kind of syllabus presents topic which are to be taught in the order of simple and more easily learnt to complex or less easily learnt in order to achieve educational progression in a nutshell these contributions made by these models have been invaluable over time in the teaching of language use in esp english for specific purposes esp is not a variety of english but an approach to the teaching of english which is concerned with tailoring the nature of the english taught according to the needs of the learners shortly after the second world war it became pronounced and was mainly known as est english for science and technology however over time english instructors also found the need to tailor the language according to the needs of members of other profession thus we have commercial english medical english e t c based on this one can then go on to say that esp focuses on the teaching of advanced or intermediate learners who need to develop the proficiency in the use of the language in order to meet up with the demands of their profession in accordance with this esp makes the learners in esp learn english through the context of their target situation they are presented with authentic language situations and thus this gives them the motivation to learn since they see the immediate relevance of english to their need also in esp one or two specific skills are taught rather than the four skills the skills which the learners are trained in are those which would be relevant to the demands of their target situation or area of specialization furthermore esp is not preoccupied with the teaching of structures alone or description of language use rather it is learning-centred that is the focus is on getting the appropriate need s of the learner s and ensuring that it is taught effectively and learnt on the other hand general english is also an approach to english language teaching elt which is concerned with the learning of english generally as a medium of communication this approach is usually encountered especially in esl environments at the primary secondary and tertiary levels of education in this approach as well the content of what is to be taught is actually very wide and that is why it spans for a long time the learners vary based on the level at which they are being taught and there is less motivation compared to esp this approach incorporates the teaching of the four communication skills listening speaking reading and writing and ensures the proficiency of the learners in these skills also it is preoccupied with the teaching of the structures of the english language and even the description of the system of the language from the above description of both approaches it can be deduced that the content of the esp syllabus is limited to that of general english since it does not teach the four skills while the latter does the learners in esp are usually older and advanced learners while in general english the learners exist in different levels esp is not preoccupied with the description of the nature or structure of the language while general english contains a great deal of it apart from these differences the similarities between both also exist in that they are both not varieties of english but approaches to the teaching elt of english in esl or efl environments also they both take care of remedial english for learners who are defective in the use of the english language lastly which is a very obvious case they both engage in the teaching of english to learners whether young or old so it could be said that though these two approaches are different they word are not disparate the origin of esp the origin of esp can be traced back to the end of the second world war in at this time there was an explosion in the activities of science technology and commerce word the need arose all over the world for a international linking language which would serve as the means of communication to people anywhere english came in promptly to suit that need since words it is the language spoken by the western world the world powers as a result of this there arose a need for the learning of the language by those who desperately needed it for the business man who needed to communicate with foreign customers for the technician who needed to read manuals written in english and so on also there was pressure word on english language instructors to tailor the language to be taught according to the specific needs of the learner because of time constraints so no matter how it was to be done these needs had to be met it would be useful also to add that the oil crises in the s also contributed to this since a good portion of the western world had to throng one word the oil-rich countries for technological and business activities so what pervaded the learning of english then was tell me what you need english for and i will tell you the english you need in other words the demands of the brave new world word led to the development of esp in addition reaction to the situation above it was observed that the earlier method of teaching english which was more like a leisurely stroll through the description of the language began to change the language instructors now had to rise to the demand of the world by providing a prompt and less time-consuming way of learning the language so there arose a revolution in linguistics the developments in the field of educational psychology which proposed a paradigm shift from the language to the learner and how s he can gain proficiency in the learning of the new language words also led to the origin of esp target needs this is a concept in esp that is used to refer to word what the learners needs linguistically to function well in his or her target situation or area of specialization this is based on the notions of necessities lacks and wants the necessities have to do with with the observable linguistic characteristics in the target situation which the learner needs in order to function well therein the lacks have to do with those linguistic features which are not yet present in the learner s repertoire when the necessity is merged with what the learner already knows the missing links would then be seen as what the learner lacks it is necessary to know specifically what the learners lack in order to avoid a case of teaching what they already know so that their motivation will not be hampered it should be noted the needs do not exist independent of the learners there could be cases where what the learner wants is in contrast with what the esp expert has diagnosed to be his or her need in such situations the trainer has to incorporate also these wants of the learner based on this an instance can be given on the training of a secretary after a careful needs analysis procedure the esp expert may discover that the one word linguistic features which the trainee needs to function well in his or her place of work would be based mainly on the writing skill writing formal letters memos ability to use correct punctuation e t c these features are the learner s necessities based on the earlier observation of the esp expert s he can then go on to carry out some diagnostic testing to know what among the earlier mentioned features the learner already knows in the example if the trainee only knows how to write formal letters the trainer will be able to recognize that other topics like writing memos and using correct punctuation amongst others are the trainee s lacks however if this trainee feels strongly that s he needs to know more on how to write formal letters this is his or her wants and should be incorporated into the syllabus learning needs this concept is concerned with how the learners will learn what they are supposed to learn after having identified the target needs of the learner the esp expert will then have the obligation to state how s he intends to achieve the learning act by the student in other words the necessary methodologies which would be used to train the students learner is of paramount importance these learning needs would hence incorporate questions like what materials would be used in the training of the learners will they be in soft or hard copies where will the training take place and how conducive is the place who will the trainers be how many are they and how many trainees will be given to each of the instructors what method s will be incorporated to facilitate easy learning will projection screens be used or other electronic devices like laptops when will the training take place what days of the week and what is the duration of each class all these and much more are the questions which attend to the learning needs of the trainees learning-centred approach the learning-centred approach is the last stage in the development of esp this evolved from the earlier approaches which focused on the language use of the learners in specific contexts of communication this approach of course shifted the focus off from the language use to the process of learning the language which the learners have set out to learn for the proponents of this approach the question they seek to answer is how can the english language be learnt in response to this the esp expert has to pay attention to the most valuable means of successfully imparting the knowledge of the language into the learner

pledges to the gods and in return the gods will give them protection and riches the events that led to the sack of owu was is that the law of the gods is that no yoruba should take a yoruba into slavery but the people of owu went to apomu market harrassed the beautiful maiden s sexually and took some back to the city also the birth of dejumo is prophesied that he would bring destruction to owu he is to be killed but erelu refused to kill him out of motherly love at last the whole blame rested on erelu for not allowing dejumo to be killed section b question captain bluntschli is a soldier who is suposed to carry ammunition but carrys chocolate the writer used humor to convey his message also we see humor when bluntschili who is inside his enemy enemies house instead of keeping alert sleeps off as raina went to call her mother catherine bluntschili who was is disguised in petkoff s coat runs away and after the war he decides to bring return the coat when louka opened the door for him catherine seeing bluntschili wanted him to disappear without her husband knowing about his visit there is humor also when petkoff came out from the library and sees bluntschili but that act made catherine unconfortable section c question the simplicity of the boy on the swing centers on his blue shirt and his short it is believed that in south africa the whites descriminate the black and all blacks are entitled to live in abject poverty the blue shirt signifies how poverty has envoded over him also we see simplicity in the boy because instead of going to a hotel he decides to be on the swing the poem is decitful deceptive it has a hidden idea outside the poem because it has alot to do with racial descrimination and apartied mother when will i wear long trousers simply means that as old as he is he enjoys wearing shorts because of the type of job he is required to do it will be of no use if he decides to wear long-trousers therefore he is seen as simple paper section a trees peoples homes burnt down and most times death but they did not know that what ever happened is as a result of nature pokuwa and have in their helplessness visited tano for a child but utterly paper drama and poetry section a african drama the city of owu was a peaceful one they were blessed economically by their gods they flourished in their economic status and also they were peagans but the people of owu wronged their gods which led to the destruction of their city there was a prophecy that a son who would be bore by the queen erelu would bring calamity but the queen refused they were told to kill the child but the queen hid him till he grew up and became a full grown man his name was dejumo a a woman named iyunoye was bethroded to okunade who was good in the work of arts they loved each other very much but iyunoye was forcefully tookn her away from him and given to prince dejumo this made okunade very sad and angry and made him sort a way to revenge on the people of owu so he left his work and joined the army the people of owu also committed a sacralage by selling their fellow to slavery to the white men it was a taboo for a yoruba to sell a fellow yoruba so when the goddess lawunmi saw this she was very angry and decided to punish the people of owu for their sins anlugbua her son tried to plead on their behalf to this mother but the goddess lawumi refused section b non-african drama no humour can be said to be what arises laugh in the minds of the audience humour can be cited in the play where a swiss machinery known as bluntschli came into the house room of a lady known as raina through the shutters and threatened her life if she shouted to alert the members of her household but she was after a long conversation between the both of them raina found out that bluntschli who is a soldier that ought to put ammunitions in his gun rather put chocolate this event made raina call him the chocolate cream soldier section c african poetry no the poem is a very simple one boy on a swing by oswald mtshali is a simple poem but is deceptive because of the strategy in which the poet wrote it there are so many poetic devices in the poem e g asonance repitition and so on which makes the poem deceptive paper section a african prose tano tano is the god to the people of brenhoma the people of brenhoma gave him supplications and also worshipped him they put their trust in tano and also called him their eminent god when a woman known as pokuwaa was barren she went to tano because she believed he would give her a child and gave him offerings and worshipped him always koramoa koramoa is a very close friend to pokuwaa and also has deep affection for pokuwaa koramoa is the second wife to koffi dafo their relationship was right from their childhood koramo was the one who got married to koffi dafo after pokuwaa and koffi dafo divorced a she always consoled pokuwaa because whenever pokuwaa thought of her barreness section b non-african prose no the playwright used symbolism in the novel santiago the old man represents jesus christ when the boy which is manolin told him his the holes wounds in his palms looked like fishless desert and they were so deep manolin represents a faithful servant he helped santiago the old him man in his fishing work the sea represents the world and its troubles the marlin fish represents the difficulties jesus went through when he was in the world

other yoruba for slavery but owu people didn t obey the instruction the owu people waged war against ile-ife and took their men took their men into slavery one of the people person that owu took from ile-ife was inyuloye the wife of okunade so they took her as a wife so lawumi was angry because the people of owu didn t keep the sacred law so she okunade who was a vengeful man that wanted to avenge not because of the love of his country but because of the love of a woman okunade left his art proffesion and joined the army and rose to the rank of a maye so the allied forces broke into owu city and killed all their men meanwhile the king of owu akinjobi and his officers fled the allied forces took over owu land and sack the city of owu humour may be defined as the creation of laughter or it initates laughter humour can be found in the case of bluntschili the fugitive a swiss machinery hired to fight for the serbs against the bulgarians rather to load his ammunitions with bullet rather it was loaded with chocolates that was why he was nicknamed the chocolate cream soldier another humour can be seen in the case of nicola the man servant who was embarrased by catherine when he brought the luggages of bluntschili and it was seen by mayor petkoff that was why catherine was angry and she embarrassed him another humour was when bluntschili came to return the coat raina shouted the chocolate cream soldier and mayor petkoff was suprised and asked catherine and raina who how the man came to be and who he was the poem the boy on the swing was written by oswald mtshali the poem is figuritive which means that it has a hidden meaning the they didn t told us who the boy is but we know that somebody is on the swing there is a theme of poverty in one of the stanza s which says his blue shirt billows in the breeze like a tattered kite whe means that the tattered and the blue shirt he was wearing shows a sign of poverty meanwhile the blacks can not afford long trousers only the white can afford the long trousers that was why he was asking his history and asking in one of the stazans which says thus when will i wear long trousers there is t loneliness w because he has nothing doing so he went to the swing to pass out time and he asked in one of the stanza s which says thus why was my father jailed tano tano is the great gods in a novel when pokuwa wants a child the gods told her to bring back and egg so that she will bear a child anybody that have any problem will go to the gods for the problem to be solved brenhoma brenhoma was known as a village in the novel and the people of brenhoma and the entire community of brenhoma were doing well when pokuwa went to the farm and saw a dead body she shouted on top of her voice the people of brenhoma come and see what is happening in the village question santiago is a fisher man and the name of the boy is manolin their relationship was a strong relationship the two of them love and care for each other manolin even help the man in carrying his fish tools to his boat manolin also buys food s drinks for the man even when the boy is told not to follow the man to stream by his father parents inspite of that he still fayose ibori to stay in prison till jan ex-delta governor s loyalists protest from godwin isenyo kaduna and edo ugbagwu former governor of delta state chief james ibori lost yesterday his battle for bail at a federal high court sitting in kaduna but his case is to get accelerated hearing he is charged with alleged corruption money laundering and other financial crimes by the economic and financial crimes commission efcc in lagos former ekiti state governor ayo fayose was arraigned for allegedly laundering n and he was ordered to be remanded in prison the court ordered that ibori be remanded in the prison till january next year when it will begin the trial proper the efcc alleges that ibori stole n billion belonging to delta state he was arraigned on a -count charge to which the former governor pleaded not guilty also charged with the former governor are miss uzoamaka okoronkwo and three companies mer engineering nig ltd bainonex limited and sagicon nigeria limited mr justice mohammed lawal shuaibu ruled that the court denied the former governor bail because there were not enough material evidence for granting the application he said in cases of this nature the onus of establishing the argument for bail rested with the defendant but the defence counsel did not fully discharge that responsibility the issue in contention is not whether the charge must be accompanied by proof of evidence but however failing to do so will make the court to exercise its discretion favourably in granting bail mr justice shuaibu said he went on but considering the duty of the accused s counsel applicants failed to make sufficient material for the use of the court in the determination of the application and also taking into account that the guidelines for consideration of bail are not water-tight the absence of proof of evidence will therefore not be a factor in determining the merits or demerits of the application this presupposes that the court will only be disposed to other considerations before delving into the merits of the application consideration is also given in the case of abiola against the federal republic of nigeria supra and the case of musa against commissioner of police nigeria weekly law report part k and it is my view that the circumstances in such cases are not the same as in the instant case in the instant case the accused applicants are arraigned before this court and the prosecution has applied for a trial date all the counts of the charge in which the accused applicants are charged are bailable offences and the next germane issues to consider are the materials placed before the court which will determine whether or not to grant bail in favour of the accused applicants the learned senior counsel mr daudu has submitted that the accused applicants will surrender themselves for trial which the prosecution debunked availability of the accused to stand trial has always been an important factor to be considered by the court in the light of the above and considering the surrounding circumstances of this case the accused applicants have not placed before the court sufficient materials with which the court will exercise its own noble discretion in granting bail before the trial the application for bail in respect of the first and second accused applicants is hereby refused and in the circumstance an accelerated trial is hereby granted that s my ruling at about a m amidst tight security the accused persons were driven into the court premises in a gray metallic peugeot expert with nasarawa registration number aa nnn mr joseph b daudu san urged the court to grant bail to the former governor since the offence was bailable saying that all the -count charges were brought under the money laundering act and and none carried death sentence according to daudu there was no proof of evidence attached to the charges to enable the judge assess the prosecution s strength of evidence the authorities are in favour of bail being granted where there is no proof but the efcc counsel mr rotimi jacobs said that the argument adduced by the defence counsel was not enough to justify his client s bail because the manner in which ibori was arrested was yet to be told he argued that the commission had facts to prove that ibori was not a man to be granted bail adding that the former governor has a criminal record mr justice shuaibu insisted that the defence counsel refused to discharge his responsibility as to why his client should be granted bail he then ordered an accelerated hearing of the case and also ordered that the former governor be in the prison custody based upon the request of the defence counsel who complained of non-accessibility to his client at the efcc custody where he ibori was initially detained daudu who said he was satisfied with the ruling said there was nothing unusual for a judge to refuse bail in lagos fayose pleaded not guilty to a -count charge of financial improprieties fayose at a federal high court was accused of laundering about n of the state s money through two companies biological concepts nigeria limited and avian specialist nigeria limited the funds were meant for the state s integrated poultry project scheme the court was told the charge was a substitute of an earlier one filed by the economic and financial crimes commission efcc last year an aide of fayose goke olatunji and businessman gbenga james standing trial in the old charge mr justice tijani abubakar inquired about the whereabout of the duo olatunji and james efcc lawyer wahab shittu said the commission had dropped all the charges against them shittu told the court that the new charge contains the old facts the old charge contained fayose s name but when it was filed he could not be prosecuted because of his constitutional immunity by discharging olatunji and james efcc may decide to use them as prosecution witnesses against fayose at about am fayose who was dressed in a white check long sleeve shirt and a light brown pair of trousers held in place by a white belt was led into the dock while in the dock he removed a brown baseball cap he wore and remained standing as the charges were read out he pleaded not guilty to them all the judge ordered that fayose be remanded in prison the prison remand was at the instance of his lawyers led by two senior advocates mallam yussuf ali and mr adebayo oba adelodun but shittu opposed the choice of prison remand alli said i ask that the accused be remanded in prison custody justice abubakar said this is the first time i am seeing an accused person opting for prison he shittu eventually agreed mr justice abubakar expressed dismay at the choice of prison remand by fayose but nevertheless granted him his wishes following his not-guilty plea shittu had asked the court to keep the accused in efcc s custody and requested for a trial date to prove the allegations preferred against fayose who was impeached last year in controversial circumstances ali made spirited efforts to make oral application for his fayose bail but mr justice abubakar advised ali against the move and told him to file a formal application for the bail ali readily accepted mr justice abubakar s suggestion and the matter was adjourned till january fayose who criticized the overzealousness of the efcc operatives in court said give me breathing space i am a former governor and a leader in nigeria give me space when i walk accord me maximum respect as a former governor fayose reminded the efcc operatives that his present circumstance was temporal this travail is temporary he said asked to comment on the charges fayose declined this is not the time to comment as you well know silence is golden he said even as he acknowledged cheers from court officials and others
us embassy boosts health reporting in nigeria about health journalists nation-wide have benefited from a media empowerment workshop held in lagos and abuja to equip them with modern skills in community health reporting as well as broaden their professional horizon the workshop which took place at planet one lagos and transcorp hilton hotel abuja was facilitated by ruyi communications limited in partnership with the public affairs section of the american embassy it attracted three american media trainers duncan moore bob hirshon and andrew holts- besides four nigerian media practitioners moji makanjuola akin jimoh funke treasure durodola and godwin odemwingie- as resource persons the worskhop focused on topics ranging from personal hygiene to sourcing health stories from non-health developments among others in her remarks at the lagos workshop the american consul-general vicki hutchinson while noting that healthy communities are the backbone of every great nation said the workshop would empower participants with the right skills and knowledge base to improve the quality accuracy and visibility of healthcare reporting writing and editing as a pivotal step to assist nigeria realise her vision goal health is now the most important foreign policy issue of our time health programs are the fastest-growing component of the u s mission s development agenda in nigeria our programs save millions of lives by supporting immunisations disease prevention and control through nutrition sanitation hygiene and other health interventions this workshop seeks to seize on a critical health diplomacy opportunity and build upon the momentum of our aid programs by improving nigeria s healthcare journalism and reporting the media s potential as a positive influence towards a healthier nigeria is the reason we are here to train and re-tool nigerian journalists we believe those of you selected for this training are the right people to affect a positive change we believe this workshop will equip you with the right skills knowledge and attitude to overcome the challenges you may face in your important role of informing the public ms hutchinson also used the opportunity to restate her home country s commitment to the global efforts against hiv aids especially as it concerns nigeria in president george bush announced the president s emergency plan for aids relief pepfar- a five-year billion multifaceted approach to combating hiv aids globally in may this year president bush announced a doubling of the initial commitment to a total of billion pepfar supports diverse prevention treatment and care programs with an emphasis on transparency and accountability for results the heart of this initiative is a partnership with the government and people of nigeria to build a locally-driven response to the pandemic nigeria is one of the pepfar s focus countries from to nigeria received more than million dollars in assistance and pepfar has achieved encouraging results such as free diagnosis and antiretroviral treatment for those infected with hiv community outreach for hiv awareness and prevention and providing care and support to children orphaned by aids as well as other vulnerable children she said over people have been reached with hiv counselling and testing pregnant women have been provided with health services for the prevention of mother-to-child transmission of hiv and over men women and children are on anti-retroviral therapy said hutchinson in his address the director general of national agency for the control of aids naca prof babatunde osotimehin took participants through the experiences challenges and discoveries with regards to aids awareness and control in nigeria during the training dr morhanson-bello of the university college hospital ibadan representing dr oladosu ojengbede led a demonstration session on the use of anti-shock garment also known as life wrap to save lives and protect women from the effects of post-partum haemorrhage thereby controlling the worrisome growth of maternal mortality in the country meanwhile the ceo of ruyi communications mr soni irabor said the media training programme was aimed at paying more attention to quality and accuracy of reporting while imbibing in the reporter the need to develop the habit of investigating and researching the facts of the stories they cover he emphasized that the training programmes are designed to complement the efforts of media organizations who pay attention to training and retraining of their professionals or those practitioners who believe in developing themselves and their practice according to one of the american trainers duncan moore the workshop was in response to the realization that reporters especially health reporters wield great power and shoulder a great burden of responsibility as they inform the public on issues that are literally matters of life and death thereby making adequate knowledge base and ethics paramount in the discharge of their noble responsibility to the society
ebonyi may not benefit from global funds the ebonyi state co-ordinator of action committee on aids ebosaca augustine nwazunku has said that with the status of health facilities the problem of the hiv aids would remain a mirage he said the state risks not benefiting from the global funds support billed to begin next year if the condition of the health facilities were not improved upon he made the remarks in abakaliki the state capital to mark this year s world aids day nwazunku explained that one of the conditions for accessing the global fund is that the benefiting state must met the standard of its health facilities most of our health facilities for now are short of that requirement so we are afraid that if something significant is not done to bring those health facilities to the stipulated standard we may miss out he stated the co-ordinator said that though access to hiv drugs in the state has improved they still have problems of service delivery especially in the areas of prevention of transmission of hiv aids from mother to child he said that the state has dropped from its per cent prevalence in to per cent this year nwazunku said that to ensure effective sensitisation of the rural populace both local action committee and the community action committee had been inaugurated in the state he regretted that the cases of children orphaned by aids is on the increase pointing out that the negative effects of the virus on the society is much and there is need for collaboration of all stakeholders the ebosaca boss lamented that though the government was doing relatively much but the private sector operators have kept a distance he praised the state government for its commitment to the war against the hiv-aids saying that a draft bill tailored to give legal lift to the ebosaca was already in the state house of assembly and has the blessing of the executive arm of the government

fake merchant navy groups seize nigeria s waters by bayo akomolafe maritime correspondent the nigerian merchant navy under the umbrella of nigerian merchant navy officers and water transportation senior staff association has raised alarm over the emergence of faceless groups masquerading as merchant navy groups already one of the leaders of the groups a welder with the defunct nigerian national shipping line nnsl who posed as a commodore benson edema has been arrested by the nigerian navy the president of the association captain thomas kemewerigha said two illegal groups have been posing as merchant navy he listed them as including the merchant navy and petroleum monitoring group which is headed by edema and the professional seafarers group operating at niger delta he said the association was worried over the grave security implication the development would have on the the nation he wondered why groups like merchant navy and petroleum monitoring corp headed by a self-styled commodore benson edema were allowed to operate despite its attempt to alert the necessary quarters until he was arrested by the navy in lagos few weeks ago another group professional seafarers is operating in the niger delta extorting money from the unsuspecting public at a press conference kemewerigha said the merchant navy is always one body in every country and that it is not a para-military group but usually committed to the welfare of seamen this body was first discovered in abuja and we being the custodian of the nigerian merchant navy alerted the authorities but no action was taken until the navy acted we don t want to believe that some powerful people in government are behind this group as they are using government property as their secretariat we want full investigation by the police on this as the property is still being opened daily in lagos for this group to continue to operate kemewerigha told journalists at the association s office in lagos at the weekend commending the effort of the nigerian maritime administration and safety agency nimasa for being able to register nigeria into the white list of the international maritime organisation imo he said the international transport federation imo the world body for seafarers had enquired about the illegal groups since according to him there could not be more than one as it will jeopardise nigeria s membership of the world maritime organisation kemewerigha said the leader of the said merchant navy corp is a pensioner of the defunct nigeria national shipping line where he was disengaged as a welder and wondered how he became an officer decorating himself as a commodore a rank which does not exist in the merchant navy kemewerigha noted that civil liberty organisation clo has not been properly educated on the issue before condemning the arrest and detention of benson edema

enugu raises panel on free maternal health childcare the enugu state government has inaugurated the state committee on free maternal and child care with dr martin chukwuike the commissioner for health as chairman in an inauguration speech at the government house enugu state governor sullivan chime who was represented by his deputy sunday onyebuchi noted that the free maternal and child health care programme in the state is a road map for accelerating maternal and child mortality reduction in line with the millennium development goals he added that the major causes of mortality in nigerian children included malaria preventable diseases particularly measles diarrhea and mal-nutrition the governor said that these deaths are closely related to poverty and ignorance the poor families being unable to afford the very little but much needed money to go to hospital for children s clinics antenatal clinics delivery and post-natal clinics are left with no choice except what they can afford he regretted that what they could afford most of the time would be going to traditional birth attendants and drug shops manned by untrained people the governor added that it was the challenge posed by this problem that the state government has decided to address he enumerated some objectives and targets of the programme which included acceleration and reduction of maternal and child mortality to attain the millennium development goals mdgs to reduce neonatal mortality rate by five per cent per annum and to reduce the incidence of malaria preventable diseases acute respiratory infections among others in his speech the chairman of the committee dr martin chukwunewike described the free medical programme as the first of its kind and a demonstration of governor chime s special interest in the welfare of women and children in the state chukwuweike who is also the state commissioner for health on behalf of other members thanked the governor for finding them fit for the job we believe that once the programme is flagged off it will reach all the state owned health institutions said chukwuweike he pledged their total commitment to the success of the programme stressing that they would work hard to ensure that the programme succeeded we will make sure that all the policies all the facilities needed for it to be well implemented would be put in place we promise that we will not fail you with the support of the committee members and the cooperation of government

president under pressure to terminate b contract with chinese firm from yusuf alli managing editor northern operation president umaru yar adua is under pressure to terminate an billion rail modernisation contract sealed by his predecessor s administration with a chinese company the nation has learnt the president is caught between advisers in government prodding him to pull the brakes on the deal and those urging restraint but chinese president hu jingtao has weighed in appealing to yar adua to retain the contract former president olusegun obasanjo s administration in october awarded the railway modernisation project to the china civil engineering construction corporation ccecc the consultancy component of the contract worth about million was awarded to technics engineering architecting marketing nigeria limited team a document obtained from the budget monitoring and price intelligence unit bmpiu of the presidency shows that the project involves the survey design construction and maintenance of kilometres of double-track standard gauge rail line from lagos to kano although the ccecc initially presented a bill of billion for the project the contract cost was revised to billion by the bmpiu but some top officials of the yar adua administration have expressed concern over the project cost and the manner in which the contract was awarded by the former administration while some alleged that the contract was inflated to billion from billion some others claimed that due process was sidelined there is however another view among technocrats including the team consultants that the cost is comparable to such a project in any part of the world the nation learnt yesterday that the project has caused a division in the yar adua s government as some ministers want the contract revoked whereas others notably top officials of the federal ministry of transportation and the nigerian railway corporation management have urged restraint finance minister shamsudeen usman and his transportation counterpart mrs diezani allison-madueke are in the vanguard of those pushing for termination of the contract the controversy had prompted president yar adua to raise an -man presidential committee on funding of the railway modernisation project our correspondent learnt that the panel headed by the minister of finance had recommended many options to the president including contract termination other options recommended by the committee according to a presidency document included reducing the scope of the contract to single track prolonging the contract duration from four to seven years phased implementation of the project to reduce the financial burden on government and sticking to the initial terms of the contract on funding a source at the presidency said since the committee submitted its report in january there has been intense pressure on the president to terminate the contract left to yar adua he wants to continue with the project when the presidential committee on funding submitted its report he took exception to the recommendation that the contract should be terminated but there has been intense lobbying by some powerful groups for contract termination in the midst of the lobbying the ministry of justice presented a brief to the president that there was no clause for termination of the contract in the agreement signed by a presidential negotiation committee headed by then private secretary to ex-president obasanjo mr stephen orosanye but the minister of transportation has countered that clause in the contract made provision for termination the lobby groups are said to be proposing a billion rehabilitation scheme for the railway which would largely be awarded as contract patronage of party loyalists presidency sources disclosed that chinese president jingtao has written a letter to his nigerian counterpart appealing that the contract be retained one source said the contract is assuming a diplomatic dimension this is the first time that the chinese president would interfere in a business matter in nigeria you can imagine the pressure on the president his predecessor obasanjo is also unhappy that a project he conceived is about to be terminated contacted on what informed the letter from the chinese president the chief coordinator of ccecc shi hongbing told our correspondent i can not talk to you on the issue i am out of the country railway workers yesterday in lagos vowed to resist further cut-back on the corporation s workforce which has already been trimmed to from the president of the nrc chapter of the senior staff association of communications transport and corporations ssactac mr abiodun lesi told the news agency of nigeria nan that repositioning the company required structural reforms not further staff downsizing lesi was reacting to allegations that the federal government has concluded plans to sack about workers of the corporation he said any further staff retrenchment would be unacceptable

fed govt to stop funding rural electrification from john ofikhenua umuahia the federal government is to stop funding rural electrification projects under the new policy it will pass the responsibility to state and local governments managing director rural electrification agency rea mr sam gekpe said yesterday in umuahia that president umar musa yar adua threatened to stop funding the projects because investment in the sector did not match the returns gekpe during a courtesy visit by rea management to abia state governor chief theodore orji said the president had noted that the projects were inconsistent and poorly managed he quoted the president as saying that he would be relieved to let the state and local governments take over the funding of the projects he said in recent times there has been a controversy the president is of the opinion that rural electrification should cease to be the concern of the federal government and that it should actually concern the state and local governments he is actually threatening to stop funding these projects one because of the performance they talked about in the previous years and how the projects had been managed and the second aspect is the inconsistency in the method of funding this project gekpe however added that the position of the national assembly differed from yar adua s the national assembly he said believes that the three tiers of government should collectively fund the projects according to gekpe the alternative way to fund the projects is by pooling resources under the rural electrification fund arrangement he said rea had proposed to raise funds locally for specific projects but stakeholders are scared due to the history of management of funds in the industry under the proposed arrangement a promoter will execute a project with an assurance that he will recoup his investment while the community where the project is located will provide the land and security for the installation alternatively a private investor could also fund percent of the project while state and local council contribute percent each of the cost gekpe said the land can be evaluated and converted as equity and that makes the communities part-owners of the scheme and when that happens and for non-viable projects we feel well that where the private partner is not interested the burden will be borne by the state and the agency here we will be proposing a partnership of percent contribution from both the state and local governments and community while rea provides percent while seeking the governor s support for the collaborative funding of rural electrification projects in abia state gekpe said there were rea projects in abia adding that of the projects had been completed he advised the state government to invest in solar energy for the electrification of its rural communities orji thanked rea for the projects it had executed adding that since power projects are capital intensive the cost should not be borne alone by the federal government the state according to orji has engaged the service of geometric company to provide electricity in rural and urban areas the company is asking the federal government to grant it tax waiver for equipment ordered for building the turbine for the project and if we are assured that shell will keep supplying gas it will be nice since it will lessen the burden on the federal government we are doing our best to reactivate all the rural projects in abia and we have already released transformers to our rural dwellers he added

in his welcome address at the th ordinary session of the authority of ecowas heads of state and government currently holding in abuja adding he was highly delighted to host the ecowas leaders in the nigerian capital city according to him the last months have witnessed a relative socio-political and economic stability within the west african region as well as a general improvement in the security situation as there at present is no active conflict in the region he expressed his optimism that there will be greater commitment to the ideals of democracy and good governance resulting not only in the consolidation of stability across the region but also the facilitation of requisite harmony of diverse domestic policies to accelerate the region s integration process he therefore called for a self appraisal on the progress of ecowas so far and consider the areas which require more attention in the efforts towards integration while various integration protocols particularly the ones on common external tariff cet free movement of goods and persons rights of residence and of establishment as well as the ecowas trade liberalisation scheme etls have had marked impact on efforts to promoting each west african country s economy president yar adua stated that non-tariff barriers to trade in the region still exists this according to him has arisen from conflicting interpretations of the protocols in different countries in dissonance with the spirit and the letter of the protocols therefore leaders of west african nations should make it a duty to make the operation of the protocols more robust and efficient as only then can the peoples development partners and international community be won to partner with ecowas in the realisation of its vision to transform the region nigerians in diaspora to vote in says iwu the independent national electoral commission inec has hinted on the possibility of extending voting rights to nigerians living abroad in the next general election in deference to a recent court decision before now it has not been possible for nigerians in diaspora to vote in the country s democratic process while receiving a delegation from angola led by that country s vice minister for territorial administration and electoral matters edeltrudes costa in abuja yesterday inec chairman prof maurice iwu said the consideration followed a recent court pronouncement in nigeria on the need to give nigerians outside the country the right to participate in the country s electoral process costa said his team was at the commission to understudy how nigeria conducted the elections that produced president umaru musa yar adua noting that angola was also desirous of having such a successful transition the inec chief told his visitors that certain legal considerations forced the commission to stop electronic voting because inec did not want to play into the hands of mischief makers who were looking for an opportunity to foment trouble but nevertheless he said the commission had been mandated to take steps towards enabling nigerians in the diaspora to participate in future elections adding that inec was already studying how to implement the directive according to him although violence has been associated with elections excessive use of money in politics as well as a wrong mindset which makes politicians see elections as a do- or- die affair are issues that still confront the conduct of polls in the country he said however that appreciable progress has been made by inec since the last general elections in for instance according to him the commission has moved away from the former practice in which voter s registration was done periodically and has now put in place a system of interactive electronic voter registration explaining that with the new arrangement voter registration is now continuous iwu said the commission would soon publish a list of those who registered in as required by law in a bid to deal with the major challenges confronting the smooth conduct of elections in the country iwu said inec had been spending more money on the education of voters and politicians themselves he said the problem militating against the electoral process would be solved by the time internal democracy was institutionalised in the parties according to iwu because the problem would be further reduced to a mere per cent if adequate preparations are made before the main election inec has taken steps to ensure that adequate preparations were made ahead of the next elections earlier costa had told the inec chairman that his delegation was in nigeria to avail itself of useful ideas as it prepares for its local administration elections
polls court okays nigerians abroad to vote a federal high court in abuja has ordered the independent national electoral commission inec to put in place relevant machinery to assist nigerians to register and vote from abroad in any election in nigeria without having to travel for that purpose delivering judgment in the suit filed by nigerians leaving abroad led by professor bolaji aluko hon hakeem bello and mr uzoma onyemaechi presiding judge justice adamu bello directed inec to approach the national assembly by sponsoring a bill for the sake of nigerians in diaspora justice bello however directed that in doing so the electoral umpire could borrow a leaf from the countries that have already adopted the external voting he predicated his judgment on the fact that the plaintiff had convinced the court that they are entitled to vote and be voted for it was the constitutional responsibility of inec to put in place the relevant machinery to assist the plaintiffs to vote from abroad the plaintiffs had dragged inec and the attorney-general of the federation and minister of justice to court saying that it was high time nigeria gave her citizens living abroad the opportunity to register and vote from abroad in any election in nigeria without having to travel for that purpose they stated that they are entitled to participate in the government of nigeria by voting for candidates of their choice pursuant to article of the african charter on human and people s rights ratification and enforcement act cap laws of the federation of nigeria the plaintiffs also averred that they are qualified for registration as voters by virtue of section c of the electoral act and sections and of the constitution they therefore demanded an order from the court directing the defendants to set up registration centres and polling stations for the purposes of registering eligible voters and voting in all the high commissions and embassies abroad plaintiffs counsel mr femi falana had submitted that by the combined effect of the article of the african charter on human and people s rights section of the nigerian constitution and section of the electoral act nigerians who are years and above living overseas are legally qualified to vote for candidates of their choice in any election conducted in nigeria falana apart from the several local and foreign authorities cited exhibited a list of member states of the united nations including african countries that have recognized external voting by their nationals challenging the suit the attorney-general and inec through their counsel messrs nelson anih and chris erhabor submitted that the nigerian embassies and high commissions can not be used for voting as they do not form part of the constituencies under which elections could be held in nigeria they further contended that they have not prevented nigerians living abroad from travelling home to participate in the electoral process
nigeria to us we are not at war in niger delta the military is not at war with the people of niger delta the federal government said yesterday explaining that the ongoing operation in the region is to flush out the criminal elements the government it would not be provoked to declare war on its own citizens it identified illegal oil bunkerers and arms dealers as those behind the restiveness in the region minister of niger delta affairs ufot ekaette and his foreign affairs counterpart ojo maduekwe made these known in washington united states us at a special session organised by the centre for strategic and international studies csis ekaette who spoke on how to resolve the niger delta crisis and bring about peace and development said the armed forces were not at war with the niger delta and would never be despite provocation by criminals posing as niger delta crusaders a statement by the special assistant media to ekaette quoted him as saying that contrary to the propaganda by the oil bunkerers and arms dealers the government was doing its best to checkmate criminal tendencies in the niger delta to pave the way for genuine peace and development the statement reads in part ekaette said that it was wrong for the same people who accused the government of not developing the niger delta to turn round and drive away development agents including contractors and foreign oil workers through kidnapping for ransom killing and destruction of facilities ekaette who pointed out that it was in a bid to significantly improve the niger delta that the federal government created the ministry of niger delta added that the government has already worked out a concrete development programme for the region the ministry said it had worked out modalities for the construction of new intra-and-inter-city roads in addition to the multi-billion niger delta coastal and east-west roads the statement added he also said that no fewer than housing units had been slated for construction in the nine states that make up the niger delta by the new ministry while the training of youths will begin next month ekaette also said an inventory of dumpsites and oil impacted sites in the niger delta region was being undertaken with a view to remedying the areas for productive uses by the communities he however appealed to the international community to support nigeria s effort to end the niger delta crisis by checking the sale of illegal of arms into the niger delta in exchange for nigeria s stolen crude oil by criminals the minister stated that the federal government was determined to end the crisis by offering amnesty to those willing to lay down their arms and be trained and re-integrated into the society for meaningful life minister of foreign affairs chief ojo maduekwe made it clear that the recent crisis in the niger delta was triggered by criminals who ambushed and killed nigerian soldiers on routine duties and said that the soldiers were not out to engage in wanton destruction of lives and property of any community maduekwe drew the attention of the audience to the fact that as a country that emerged from a very painful civil war nigeria would not allow any group or individual to plunge it into avoidable conflict and appealed to the international community to allow nigeria to take steps to resolve the problems posed by criminals in the niger delta
cbn to banks disclose stake in transcorp virgin from the central bank of nigeria cbn yesterday came a directive to all banks and discount houses submit details of your exposures including commercial papers cps and bankers acceptances to transnational corporation plc transcorp and virgin nigeria limited vnl besides the directive contained in a june circular requested for sold and bought counter-parties in respect of both companies the circular signed by cbn s acting director banking supervision dan eke gave the banks up till june to comply with the directive a bankers acceptance is created when a time draft drawn on a bank usually to finance shipment or temporary storage of goods is stamped accepted by the bank by accepting the draft the bank makes an unconditional promise to pay the holder of the draft a stated amount at a specified date acceptances are traded at a discount from face value on the secondary market banker s acceptances are similar to treasury-bills and are often used in money market funds commercial papers are unsecured short-term debt instruments issued by banks for the financing of accounts receivable inventories and meeting short-term liabilities commercial paper is not usually backed by any form of collateral so only firms with high-quality debt ratings will easily find buyers without having to offer a substantial discount higher cost for the debt issue on june the national council on privatisation ncp revoked the sale of nigerian telecommunications nitel and mobile telecommunications mtel to transcorp transcorp had acquired the firms for m in august transcorp s acquisition of nitel mtel stirred controversy in the land the workers kicked against the transaction claiming that it was against the nation s interest many even perceived that transcorp acted as a front for former president olusegun obasanjo to buy the public utility their suspicion was fuelled by obasanjo s claudestine investment of n million in transcorp through a blind trust during the period transcorp that ran nitel mtel it neither pay staff salary for months nor settle pensioners claim a few weeks ago transcorp managing director chief executive tom iseghohi was arrested over what many believe is not unconnected with the nitel mtel crisis an abuja federal high court has ordered that he be remanded in custody the deal between the government and virgin atlantic seems to have gone awry with the planned change in the name of virgin nigeria hinting of the planned change in lagos about days ago virgin nigeria managing director captain dapo olumide said since its inception in the company s staff had been undergoing training with virgin atlantic they have taught us how to work nigerians have acquired those skills and we are going to put them to work we have done it before and we are going to do it again virgin atlantic can not be here forever the longer virgin nigeria remains under the umbrella of virgin atlantic the longer it will take us to chart our halliburton aondoakaa writes nba president on probe status the federal government is yet to identify nigerians who benefited from the million halliburton bribery scandal the attorney-general of the federation agf and minister of justice mr michael aondoakaa san made the clarification in a letter to the president of the nigerian bar association nba chief oluwarotimi akeredolu san on the status of the ongoing probe into the scam in the letter which was exclusively obtained by the nation the agf said nigeria is exploring mutual legal assistance treaty with the united states and switzerland to unmask the bribe-takers the letter reads i wish to draw your esteem attention to the above subject and to intimate you on the efforts being made by the federal government of nigeria to obtain relevant information about the involvement of nigerians in the halliburton bribery scandal it will be recalled that the federal government recently commenced investigation into a case of suspected criminal conspiracy among nigerian government officials other nigerians all yet to be identified and foreign nationals to obtain a contract for construction of liquefied natural gas lng facilities in exchange for gratifications the investigation evolved from the case of united states of america v stanley-case no -cr- - us district court for the southern district of texas as a result of the conviction of one albert stanley jackson a us citizen resident in houston texas for violations of the foreign corrupt practices act fcpa according to the court records on the rd of september stanley a former officer and director of kellogg brown and root inc a global engineering and construction services company based in houston texas pleaded guilty to conspiracy to violate the fcpa in connection with a scheme to bribe nigerian government officials in order to obtain engineering procurement and construction contracts worth over us billion by pleading guilty to the charges mr stanley acknowledged that he and his co-conspirators wilfully agreed to make improper payments to nigerian officials to ensure support that the joint venture obtain and retain contracts in connection with the lng project mr stanley was sentenced to seven years in prison in addition to payment of restitution in the amount of us although the court records that could reveal the identity of the nigerians involved in the bribery scandal is yet to be de-classified by the us courts the federal government is determined to prosecute all the participants in this case of corruption wherever they may be located to the full extent of the nigerian law in order to ensure that evidence necessary to sustain the charge is obtained a request for mutual legal assistance in criminal matters has been made to the us attorney-general and the government of switzerland mr president has also constituted an investigation panel comprising the director-general of state security services sss director-general national intelligence agency nia and the chairman of the economic and financial crimes commission efcc with the inspector-general of police ig as chairman government expects to receive the necessary cooperation from the various countries whose assistance has been sought it is also our hope that the investigation panel will be able to uncover the evidence required to prosecute those involved in the bribery scandal it has become pertinent to inform you of these developments and to solicit your support as well as that of the bar in complementing the efforts so far made by the government this is in view of the realisation that the bar under your able leadership can play a key role in complementing and supporting ongoing efforts to rid our society of corruption it was learnt that the government decided to reach out to the nba leadership to prevail on its members for understanding when the list of the bribe-takers is released the agf who also spoke with the nation said the government would collaborate with the nba on the probe aondoakaa said i have written officially to the nba president who has acknowledged it for collaboration because they have to give us all the assistance this is something that is very germane and when we gathered this evidence we need their support in as much as the lawyers need to make their fees there is the need to take national interest into consideration and the response of the nba president is very cordial also yesterday the all nigerian peoples party anpp urged the okiro-led panel not to compromise the party in a statement signed by its national publicity secretary emma eneukwu said it learnt that efforts by the panel to get the list of nigerian officials involved in the bribe have been futile anpp said though the us about a month ago pledged to allow the panel access to some documents that might hasten its work the party however said it was doubtful if the panel followed the right step in getting the documents the party advised president umaru yar adua to compel the okiro-led panel to get the documents from us at all costs it said former president olusegun obasanjo and former head of state general abdulsalami abubakar rtd under whose tenure the scam was perpetrated should be interrogated by the panel
